---
title: "05 — Weak-label, audit, and build train/dev/test"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, message = TRUE, warning = TRUE)
PROJECT_ROOT <- Sys.getenv("CAPLAN_ROOT")
if (PROJECT_ROOT == "") PROJECT_ROOT <- rprojroot::find_root(rprojroot::has_file("_config.R"))
knitr::opts_knit$set(root.dir = PROJECT_ROOT)
source(file.path(PROJECT_ROOT, "_config.R"))
source(file.path(PROJECT_ROOT, "R", "helpers.R"))
library(arrow); library(data.table); library(dplyr)
set.seed(SEED)
```

Turns the labeling queue into supervised splits: (1) LLM weak-labels via `python/llm_label.py`,
(2) a **human audit** of a stratified subset establishes reliability, (3) build `train/dev/test`
split **by title + year** to prevent leakage. See docs/CODEBOOK.md.

## 1. Run LLM weak-labeling

```{r llm-label}
queue   <- file.path(DATA_PANELS, "label_queue.parquet")
labels  <- file.path(DATA_PANELS, "llm_labels.parquet")
# Requires GEMINI_API_KEY (or ANTHROPIC_API_KEY) in the environment.
if (!file.exists(labels)) {
  exit <- system2(PYTHON_EXE, args = c(
    shQuote(file.path(PROJECT_ROOT, "python", "llm_label.py")),
    "--in",  shQuote(queue), "--out", shQuote(labels),
    "--provider", "gemini", "--model", "gemini-2.0-flash",
    "--text-col", "text", "--id-col", "article_id"
  ), stdout = "", stderr = "", wait = TRUE)
  if (exit != 0) warning("llm_label.py exited ", exit)
}
lab <- as.data.table(read_parquet(labels))
message("LLM labels: ", nrow(lab), "; parse_ok: ", sum(lab$parse_ok, na.rm = TRUE))
```

## 2. Define binary targets

```{r targets}
q <- as.data.table(read_parquet(queue))
d <- merge(q, lab, by = "article_id", all.x = TRUE)

d[, relevance := fifelse(is.na(relevance), 0L, as.integer(relevance))]
for (b in c("anti_market", "anti_foreign", "make_work", "pessimistic")) {
  st <- paste0(b, "_stance")
  d[, (paste0("y_", b)) := fifelse(get(st) %in% "endorse", 1L, 0L)]
}
# Drop unusable OCR for training
d <- d[is.na(quality) | quality != "unusable"]
message("Label base rates:")
print(d[, lapply(.SD, mean), .SDcols = patterns("^y_|relevance")])
```

## 3. Human audit subset (reliability)

```{r audit}
audit_path <- file.path(DATA_PANELS, "audit_human.csv")
if (!file.exists(audit_path)) {
  # Stratify the audit by bias-candidate and era so rare positives are checked.
  d[, era := (year %/% 10) * 10]
  audit <- d[, .SD[sample(.N, min(.N, 30))], by = .(sample_reason, era)]
  cols <- c("article_id", "text", "relevance",
            paste0("y_", c("anti_market","anti_foreign","make_work","pessimistic")))
  out <- audit[, ..cols]
  out[, `:=`(h_relevance = NA_integer_,
             h_anti_market = NA_integer_, h_anti_foreign = NA_integer_,
             h_make_work = NA_integer_, h_pessimistic = NA_integer_)]
  fwrite(out, audit_path)
  message("Wrote ", nrow(out), " rows to audit_human.csv.")
  message("TODO(human): fill the h_* columns per CODEBOOK, then re-run this chunk.")
} else {
  a <- fread(audit_path)
  if (!all(is.na(a$h_relevance))) {
    # Cohen's kappa on stance agreement (LLM vs human) per bias
    kappa <- function(x, y) {
      ok <- !is.na(x) & !is.na(y); x <- x[ok]; y <- y[ok]
      po <- mean(x == y); pe <- sum(table(x)/length(x) * table(y)/length(y))
      (po - pe) / (1 - pe)
    }
    for (b in c("anti_market","anti_foreign","make_work","pessimistic")) {
      message(sprintf("  kappa[%s] = %.2f", b,
                      kappa(a[[paste0("y_", b)]], a[[paste0("h_", b)]])))
    }
    message("Target kappa >= 0.6 before trusting LLM labels at scale (CODEBOOK §5).")
  }
}
```

## 4. Train/dev/test split (by title + year, no leakage)

```{r split}
grp_col <- if ("lccn" %in% names(d)) "lccn" else "newspaper_name"
d[, grp := paste0(get(grp_col), "_", (year %/% 5) * 5)]   # title x 5-year block
groups <- unique(d$grp); g <- sample(groups)
n <- length(g)
train_g <- g[1:floor(.7*n)]; dev_g <- g[(floor(.7*n)+1):floor(.85*n)]; test_g <- g[(floor(.85*n)+1):n]

splits <- list(train = d[grp %in% train_g], dev = d[grp %in% dev_g], test = d[grp %in% test_g])
for (nm in names(splits)) {
  keep <- c("article_id", "text", "relevance",
            paste0("y_", c("anti_market","anti_foreign","make_work","pessimistic")))
  write_parquet(splits[[nm]][, ..keep], file.path(DATA_PANELS, paste0(nm, ".parquet")))
  message(nm, ": ", nrow(splits[[nm]]))
}
```
